macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Struggles Below 6800 Amid Energy Surge and Sector Weakness
items.summary: The S&P 500 fumbled support at 6800, closing down 0.13% with choppy trading, while energy stocks led gains due to surging crude oil prices; financials and consumer staples weakened significantly.

items.title: Major Indexes Show Relative Weakness; NASDAQ and Russell 2000 Under Pressure
items.summary: S&P 500 down 0.13%, NASDAQ and Russell 2000 (IWO) rejected moving averages with lower highs and lows; market breadth deteriorating amid elevated volatility.

items.title: Crude Oil Prices Surge on Geopolitical Tensions and Shipping Issues
items.summary: WTI crude finished at $87/barrel and Brent above $90, driven by volatility from Iran conflict, Strait of Hormuz disruptions, and potential US oil reserve taps; energy sector (XLE) outperforms.

items.title: Rising Treasury Yields Signal Fewer Fed Rate Cuts Amid Inflation Fears
items.summary: 10-year Treasury yields rose as markets price in reduced Fed rate cuts due to commodity-driven inflation concerns from oil and soft commodities; VIX remains elevated around 24.
items.source: Market Misbehavior with Dave Keller, CMT

items.title: Trump Signals Potential US Oil Reserve Release as Production Restarts
items.summary: President Trump indicated US may tap strategic oil reserves amid rising prices; California offshore oil production restart news boosted energy stocks late in the session.

items.title: Iran Conflict Escalates Oil Volatility; Worst-Case Scenarios for Markets
items.summary: Wall Street closed weakly with Dow most affected; heightened oil volatility from Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz issues raises fears of prolonged war and soaring energy prices.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Wells Fargo Files Trademark for WFUSD Stablecoin
items.summary: Wells Fargo has filed a crypto-related trademark application for WFUSD, indicating a major push into cryptocurrency and blockchain services. This move signals traditional finance's deepening involvement in digital assets.

items.title: Mastercard Launches New Crypto Partner Program
items.summary: Mastercard announced a new Crypto Partner Program to support blockchain and crypto integrations. The rollout initially covers 32 Ethereum Virtual Machine compatible blockchains including Ethereum, BNB Smart Chain, Polygon, and others.

items.title: Ledger Partners with Babylon for Trustless Bitcoin Vaults
items.summary: Ledger signers will be used for Babylon's BTC vaults, allowing Bitcoin holders to lock tokens into programmable contracts while maintaining self-custody. This enhances security and adoption for Bitcoin staking.

items.title: Geopolitical Tensions and Oil Prices Threaten Crypto Markets
items.summary: Rising oil prices due to geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and Middle East conflicts are creating supply shocks, potentially fueling inflation and impacting crypto prices. Analysts warn of a possible market correction similar to January's Bitcoin drop from $90K to $60K.

items.title: Strive Asset Management Buys $50M in STRC for High Yields
items.summary: Strive is purchasing $50 million worth of STRC, a low-risk project offering 11.5% yields compared to 3.7% on US 10-year Treasuries. This reflects growing institutional interest in crypto yield strategies.

items.title: Fund Launches Zcash Mining Pool Amid Token Recovery
items.summary: A new fund has launched a Zcash mining pool to mutualize efforts and boost mining efficiency. This comes as Zcash token shows signs of recovery after recent declines.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: UC San Diego Develops Brain-Inspired Neuromorphic Computing Platform for Energy-Efficient AI
items.summary: Researchers at UC San Diego have created a neuromorphic computing platform that integrates memory and computation on a single chip, mimicking neural network interactions to boost pattern recognition speed and accuracy for tasks like speech recognition and epileptic seizure detection. The system achieves high energy efficiency at 0.2 nanojoules per operation, ideal for edge AI in wearables and sensors.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Model to Tackle Context and Cost Issues in Autonomous AI Agents
items.summary: NVIDIA has introduced a new model addressing key challenges in autonomous agents related to context management and operational costs, enhancing AI capabilities as noted in recent coverage.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Highguard Multiplayer Shooter Shuts Down Permanently on March 12, 2026
items.summary: The free-to-play hero shooter Highguard, launched less than two months ago on January 26 for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S, will shut down today after failing to sustain a player base despite initial 2 million players. Developers Wildlight Entertainment are releasing a final update with new content before closure.

items.title: Global Video Game Market Valued at ~USD 187 Billion with Strong Growth Potential
items.summary: Ken Research reports the global video game industry is valued at approximately USD 187 billion, highlighting robust long-term growth prospects amid ongoing market expansion.

items.title: Epic Games Names Elon University's Game Design Minor 'Unreal Academic Partner'
items.summary: Epic Games has recognized Elon University's game design minor as an 'Unreal Academic Partner,' granting students and faculty free access to Unreal Engine resources, training, and assets to enhance curriculum and professional development.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Synopsys Launches New Hardware-Assisted Verification Platforms and Electronics Digital Twins at Converge 2026
items.summary: Synopsys opened its flagship conference Converge 2026 with CEO Sassine Ghazi announcing new hardware-assisted verification platforms, software-defined capabilities, and the Electronics Digital Twins Platform for silicon-to-systems design and full-system validation. Collaborations with AMD, Intel, Microsoft, and NVIDIA were highlighted, including faster EDA tool access on Microsoft platforms powered by AMD.

items.title: NJ Innovation Evergreen Fund Invests $4.65M in Five Tech and Biotech Startups
items.summary: The New Jersey Economic Development Authority awarded $4.65 million from the NJ Innovation Evergreen Fund to startups including TranscendAP for AI-powered accounts payable automation, Lula Commerce for retail delivery management, and Synchrony Medical for chronic lung disease treatments. Investments support growth in software, cleantech, and medtech sectors.

items.title: MyOme and Natera Launch Zenith Portfolio with Long-Read Methylation Analysis for Rare Disease Diagnostics
items.summary: MyOme debuted the Zenith portfolio in partnership with Natera at ACMG 2026, integrating methylation signatures via long-read sequencing into whole-genome analysis to improve rare disease diagnostics and reduce diagnostic delays. The platform enhances exome and genome testing through Natera's clinical network.
